text.title: Delivery Maturity Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Delivery Maturity Rubric must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Score each PMBoK 7 performance domain on a 1-5 scale with explicit behaviour anchors. Never accept a numeric score without a quoted behaviour from the past quarter.

rationale.source: PMI PMBoK Guide 7th edition / CMMI v2.0 maturity scales

rule.rationale: opinion-only scoring is the dominant rubric-failure mode.

rule.statement: Calibrate scores against ≥3 named delivery events (release, incident, audit). A score without evidence in the last 90 days is downgraded to the lower band.
Score the 8 domains independently. Never collapse Team and Delivery into a 'culture' score — they have different remediation paths.

rationale.source: PMBoK 7 explicitly separates the domains because remediation is domain-specific

rule.rationale: PMBoK 7 explicitly separates the domains because remediation is domain-specific.

rule.statement: Publish the rubric BEFORE the review window opens. Mid-review rubric changes invalidate the cycle.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Annual delivery-process maturity review Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
